commit.message:
Add README with architecture overview, setup, and API docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement notification system with server cluster, workers, emulators, and load testing

- Notification server: REST API, input validation, template rendering, Redis caching, RabbitMQ routing
- Notification worker: 4 dedicated queue consumers (APNS/FCM/SMS/EMAIL) with retry logic
- Third-party emulator: simulates delivery services with 95% success rate
- Device emulator: receives notifications, WebSocket broadcast, REST stats
- Load client: seeds test data, multi-threaded load test with p95/p99 reporting
- Infrastructure: PostgreSQL, Redis, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, Grafana with pre-provisioned dashboard
- Docker Compose with service health checks and replicas
- 27 unit tests covering validation, services, controllers, consumers
- Component diagram in drawio format

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Gemini JSON parsing in auto-research + force JSON response mode

- Add responseMimeType: application/json to Gemini price intelligence call
- Add JSON repair for trailing commas, unquoted keys, single quotes
- Add partial extraction fallback for badly formatted responses
- Fixes "Expected ',' or ']'" errors when researching new cities

Deploy mirror to Pi: picamera2, portrait display, Snapchat-style tools, black idle

commit.message:
fix(ci): use shell env vars instead of template expressions in run blocks

Zizmor auto-fix converted ${{ env.IMAGE_NAME }} to ${IMAGE_NAME} (shell
var) but left ${{ env.REGISTRY }} as a template expression, causing
inconsistency. Worse, the printf in docker-merge used single quotes
around ${IMAGE_NAME} preventing shell expansion.

Fix by using ${REGISTRY}/${IMAGE_NAME} consistently as shell env vars
in all run blocks, since both are workflow-level env vars.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: navbar text colors, smooth scroll, focus toggle, menu item borders

- Navbar: use usePathname to always show solid nav on non-home pages
- Navbar: fix text/hamburger colors based on showSolid state
- Navbar: smooth scroll to anchor sections on homepage click
- Navbar: navigate to /#anchor from other pages
- FocusToggle: accept onDark prop — white semi-transparent style over hero
- FocusToggle: remove invert hack, use explicit color logic
- MenuTabs: accent-colored top border on menu item cards
- globals.css: add html { scroll-behavior: smooth }

commit.message:
Add Stab talent UI indicators: proc floaters, CD reset flash, buff pills

Players now get visual feedback for talent procs and effects:
- Purple damage floaters + combat log entries for proc damage (Venom Burst, Counter Stab, etc.)
- Blue flash on skill button when cooldown is reset by a proc (Relentless Pursuit, Reactive Footwork)
- Teal combat log entry when debuffs spread on kill (Toxic Transfer)
- Amber pill badge showing ramping stacks (Lethal Rhythm)
- Colored buff pills with countdown for temp buffs (Predator, Shadow Form, Frenzy, etc.)

Engine changes are purely additive optional fields on CombatTickResult — no behavioral changes.

commit.message:
feat(forge): wire SupervisorAgent into CLI as default task executor

do_task() now uses SupervisorAgent for intelligent multi-agent delegation
by default. The LLM analyzes the task, delegates to specialized sub-agents
(planner/builder/tester/reviewer/architect/documenter) in parallel, and
synthesizes results. YAML workflows preserved via --workflow flag.

- Add process_message() public entry point on SupervisorAgent
- Add get_supervisor() CLI factory with budget/convergence/bridge wiring
- Refactor do_task() to default to agent delegation, --workflow for YAML
- Extract _run_yaml_workflow() for legacy workflow path
- 16 new tests for process_message, get_supervisor, and CLI agent mode

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: animus
repository.description: An exocortex architecture for personal cognitive sovereignty

commit.message:
fix: end-to-end user journey - agent loop, auth, Web UI, infrastructure, skills

Critical: Create Agent Loop bridge (the system was completely non-functional)
- New agent-loop.ts bridges SessionManager → LLM Router → WebSocket response
- Messages now flow: user input → Redis pub/sub → LLM request → brpop response → output publish
- SessionManager.sendMessage() now persists user messages in session_messages table
- Sessions API supports synchronous responses (subscribe before send, 60s timeout)
- GET /api/sessions/:id/messages endpoint for conversation history retrieval

Critical: Fix authentication and Web UI
- Add GET /api/auth/me endpoint (Web UI login was completely broken)
- WebSocket now authenticates via httpOnly cookies (fallback to ?token= for CLI)
- WebSocket accepts ChatPage message format ({ type: 'user_message', content })
- Add Registration page (RegisterPage.tsx) with /register route
- Fix MFA URL: /auth/mfa/verify → /auth/totp/verify
- Fix role mapping: admin → workspace_admin across all Web UI components
- Fix all Admin UI API endpoints (/admin/agents → /agents, etc.)
- Fix manifest editor to handle { manifests: [...] } response shape
- Fix ChatPage agent list to handle { agents: [...] } response

Critical: Fix infrastructure
- Create s6-overlay user/contents.d bundle (services were never starting)
- Add SESSION_COOKIE_SECRET, JWT_SECRET, REDIS_SOCKET, DATABASE_SOCKET env overrides
- Unify Makefile: init generates .env, up/down use docker-compose consistently
- Copy honorclaw.yaml template and honorclaw-skills/ into Docker image
- Fix GEMINI_API_KEY → GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY env var name

Skills system:
- Skills install now loads from honorclaw-skills/ bundles (manifest + system prompt)
- Skills search falls back to bundle directory on empty DB results
- GET /api/skills/available lists all bundled skills
- Add system_prompt and description columns to skills table

CLI:
- Add `honorclaw login` command (email/password prompt, token persistence)
- Add `honorclaw logout` command
- Add `honorclaw skills available` command
- Login endpoint now returns tokens in JSON body (not just cookies)
- Chat command shows spinner, handles sync responses, falls back to polling
- Fix API client 401 error messages (expired vs missing token)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: honorclaw
repository.description: HonorClaw is a fully open-source platform for deploying, managing, and securing AI agents in enterprise environments. It matches the feature set of platforms like OpenClaw (agents, memory, tool calling, multi-agent orchestration) while adding enterprise-grade security controls baked into every layer.

commit.message:
Refactor effects to shared common.py, fix broken effects, add About dialog

- Extract shared utilities (config caching, palette LUT, draw_frame, frame_sleep,
  laplacian operators, blur) into effects/common.py, eliminating ~700 lines of
  duplicated code across all 19 effects
- Fix wave_interference: switch to toroidal boundaries, broad source injection,
  noise-seeded grid, and percentile-based color scaling for visible patterns
- Fix physarum: increase buffer scale 2→4, add angle jitter to prevent agent
  convergence, use sqrt normalization to reveal trail networks
- Fix cyclic_cellular: reduce states 14→8 for coherent wave fronts on 6×16 grid
- Add About dialog (about_window.py) with avatar, version, GitHub link
- Add capture_gif.py tool for headless GIF capture of effects
- Recapture GIFs for arc_sweep, lightning_strike, cyclic_cellular,
  wave_interference, and physarum
- Update EFFECTS.md descriptions to match new parameters

Fix: focus trap re-running on every render causing input focus loss

Use a ref for the escape callback so the main effect only runs on mount,
preventing items[0].focus() from stealing focus on every keystroke.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add tmux workspace support for session isolation

Sessions are now namespaced by workspace via KONSOLAI_WORKSPACE env var:

Session name format: konsolai-{workspace}-{profile}-{id}
  New:    konsolai-default-Claude-06a8ac8b
  Legacy: konsolai-Claude-06a8ac8b (treated as workspace "default")

Key changes:
- TmuxManager::currentWorkspace() reads KONSOLAI_WORKSPACE (default: "default")
- TmuxManager::buildSessionName() includes workspace in template
- TmuxManager::workspaceFromSessionName() parses workspace from name
  (handles both new and legacy formats)
- listKonsolaiSessions() filters by current workspace
- listKonsolaiSessionsAsync() filters by current workspace
- Isolated test runner sets KONSOLAI_WORKSPACE=test

Backward compatibility:
- Legacy sessions (konsolai-Claude-{id}) are detected by segment count
  and treated as workspace "default"
- Profile names are typically capitalized (Claude), workspace names lowercase

New tests (5):
- testCurrentWorkspaceDefault
- testBuildSessionNameIncludesWorkspace
- testWorkspaceFromSessionName_NewFormat
- testWorkspaceFromSessionName_LegacyFormat
- testWorkspaceFromSessionName_TestWorkspace

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: waitForConnection が接続済み状態を検知できないバグを修正 (#13)

createConnectionStateObservable() は状態「変化」のみ通知するため、
接続確立済みの場合は新たな 'connected' イベントが来ず、
waitForConnection が常に 3 秒タイムアウトまで待機していた。

これにより completeOnTimeout(3s) と競合し、2 ページ目以降の
ユーザーページでリレーへのリクエストが送信されず受賞バッジが
空と表示されるバグが発生していた。

_connected フラグを追加し、接続済みなら即座に resolve するよう修正。

https://claude.ai/code/session_01S5ooyubU8meqMb4LFyVjrp

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(ci): remove release trigger from docs workflow (#13)

The github-pages environment only permits deployments from the main
branch, not from tag refs. The release trigger fired from v0.4.0 and
was rejected by the environment protection rule.

The release trigger was redundant anyway — docs are deployed on every
push to main, which always happens before a release is cut.

commit.message:
perf: optimización masiva de carga del sitio

Imágenes:
- Convierte 6 PNG de galería a WebP: 7.8 MB → 420 KB (-94%)
- Convierte avatar.jpg a WebP: 103 KB → 84 KB
- Agrega decoding=async + fetchpriority=high al avatar

JavaScript:
- React.lazy() para Projects, About, Skills, Contact (below-fold)
- Vite manualChunks: vendor-react, vendor-motion, vendor-icons separados
- Bundle inicial: 280 KB → 20 KB

Fuentes:
- Elimina @import duplicado en CSS (solo carga desde HTML)
- Reduce pesos de fuente: 8 variantes → 5 (Inter 300/400/600, Jakarta 600/700)
- Preload de avatar.webp en index.html

feat: persistent notification log for offline events

Server: PlayerNotification model stores up to 100 events per player.
tick.py writes mission_completed, trade_mission_completed, contract_
completed/failed/offered, rig_broken events to DB. GET /game/notifications
returns unread events and marks them read.

Client: server_backend.get_notifications() fetches on first login.
main_ui emits server_notifications_received. hq_tab prepends offline
events to the Activity log with color-coded severity and a divider line.

Also marks Mission Queue and Contracts with Deadlines as ✅ in FEATURES.md.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add cross-market delta hedging and position merge harvesting

RN1 never sold positions directly — 35% of their $20M profit came from
merges. This adds two new mechanisms:

1. Position merge harvesting: every cycle scans open positions for
   profitable synthetic sells (buy opposite + merge for $1.00)
2. Cross-market delta hedging: groups positions by event, tracks net
   delta, buys underpriced opposite outcomes to reduce exposure
